mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate near-term household demand strength that lifts expected revenues for large-cap firms, so higher 3-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value for 2018-05 was 501971.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate in walk-forward tests over at least 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so an upward FEDFUNDS crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month high
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value for 2018-06 was 1.82 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS breakouts are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad income growth without labor-market deterioration, raising expected consumption and earnings, so the joint signal predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2018-06 was 148912.0 and UNRATE was 4.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger has a 60-day forward return hit rate below the unconditional positive-rate benchmark across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer inflation together with a rising policy rate signals pressure on margins and discount rates, so joint increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its trailing 12-month median and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2018-06 was 250.857 and FEDFUNDS was 1.82 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increases has nonnegative estimated effect on 60-day returns in walk-forward regressions for two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations, so HOUST downside breaks predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2018-05 was 1350.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break triggers produce average 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional 60-day average or positive returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, so stronger INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the signal is observed.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value for 2018-05 was 107.3006 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum has a negative mean 5-day forward S&P 500 return or a rank IC below 0.00 in three consecutive annual walk-forward evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A fast rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, so an upward UNRATE crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp from its trailing 3-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2018-06 was 4.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE upward-crossing triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or if downside capture is not greater than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already low and the policy rate is rising, the expansion is late-cycle and monetary tightening has less labor slack to absorb it, so low UNRATE with rising FEDFUNDS predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 4.0 and FEDFUNDS rises at least 0.50pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2018-06 was 4.0 and FEDFUNDS was 1.82 as of the decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with low unemployment
failure_condition: Reject if the low-UNRATE and rising-FEDFUNDS interaction has a positive mean 60-day S&P 500 return or fails to underperform the unconditional 60-day return average in walk-forward testing.
